-{"id":"bd-be4n","title":"Implement worktree merge and push workflow","description":"Task: Implement Worktree Merge and Push

Purpose
-------
After quality gates pass, merge worktree changes back to main branch
and push to remote. This is the final step of the Apply phase.

Workflow
--------
1. Verify quality gates passed
2. Checkout main branch in worktree
3. Merge review branch
4. Push to remote
5. Clean up worktree

Implementation
--------------

push_worktree_changes()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local wt_path="$2"
  local plan_file="$wt_path/.ru/review-plan.json"
  
  # Get branch info
  local wt_branch base_ref
  wt_branch=$(jq -r .git.branch "$plan_file")
  base_ref=$(jq -r .git.base_ref "$plan_file")
  
  # Get main repo path
  local main_repo
  main_repo=$(get_main_repo_path "$repo_id")
  
  log_step "Merging changes for $repo_id"
  
  # Fetch worktree branch to main repo
  git -C "$main_repo" fetch "$wt_path" "$wt_branch:$wt_branch"
  
  # Checkout base and merge
  git -C "$main_repo" checkout "$base_ref"
  if \! git -C "$main_repo" merge --ff-only "$wt_branch"; then
    log_error "Cannot fast-forward merge, manual resolution needed"
    return 1
  fi
  
  # Push
  if \! git -C "$main_repo" push; then
    log_error "Push failed"
    return 1
  fi
  
  log_success "Pushed changes for $repo_id"
  
  # Clean up worktree branch
  git -C "$main_repo" branch -d "$wt_branch"
  
  # Record push
  record_push_completed "$repo_id" "$wt_branch"
  
  return 0

Merge Strategy
--------------
Default: --ff-only (fast-forward only)
- Safest, requires linear history
- Fails if main has new commits
- User must resolve manually

Alternative: --no-ff
- Creates merge commit
- Works with diverged history
- Configurable per-repo

Pre-Push Verification
---------------------
verify_push_safe()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Check tests passed
  local tests_ok
  tests_ok=$(jq -r .git.tests.ok "$plan_file")
  if [[ "$tests_ok" \!= "true" ]]; then
    log_error "Tests did not pass, refusing to push"
    return 1
  fi
  
  # Check no unanswered questions
  local unanswered
  unanswered=$(jq "[.questions[] | select(.answered \!= true)] | length" "$plan_file")
  if [[ $unanswered -gt 0 ]]; then
    log_error "$unanswered unanswered questions, refusing to push"
    return 1
  fi
  
  return 0

Rollback Support
----------------
If push fails or needs reverting:

rollback_worktree_merge()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local main_repo
  main_repo=$(get_main_repo_path "$repo_id")
  
  # Get the commit before merge
  local before_merge
  before_merge=$(git -C "$main_repo" rev-parse HEAD~1)
  
  # Reset to before merge
  git -C "$main_repo" reset --hard "$before_merge"
  
  log_warn "Rolled back merge for $repo_id"

Push Recording
--------------
record_push_completed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local branch="$2"
  
  update_review_state "
    .repos[\"$repo_id\"].last_push = \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\" |
    .repos[\"$repo_id\"].last_push_branch = \"$branch\"
  "

Testing
-------
- Verify ff-only merge works
- Verify merge fails gracefully on conflict
- Verify push records in state
- Test rollback procedure
- Verify worktree cleanup

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Merges worktree to main cleanly
- [ ] Pushes only with explicit --push
- [ ] Fails safely on merge conflict
- [ ] Records push in state
- [ ] Cleans up worktree branch
- [ ] Rollback procedure works","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:11.392465319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:11.392465319-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-be4n","depends_on_id":"bd-vcr9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.539768997-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bxii","title":"Phase 4: TUI (Dashboard, Drill-down, Keyboard Shortcuts)","description":"Phase 4: TUI (Terminal User Interface)

Overview
--------
Build the human interface for reviewing aggregated questions, monitoring
session progress, and making decisions across all repos.

Why TUI?
--------
- Centralized view of all pending questions
- Quick keyboard-driven decisions
- Session monitoring at a glance
- Drill-down for context when needed
- Works over SSH, in tmux, etc.

Components
----------

4.1 Basic Mode (gum-based)
   Fallback when advanced TUI not available:
   - gum style for boxes and formatting
   - gum choose for option selection
   - gum confirm for yes/no
   - Sequential question presentation

4.2 Main Dashboard
   Full-screen TUI with panels:
   - PENDING QUESTIONS: Priority-sorted list
   - ACTIVE SESSIONS: State and progress
   - SUMMARY: Stats and metrics
   - Footer: Keyboard shortcuts

4.3 Drill-down View
   Detailed view for single question:
   - Full context from agent
   - Issue/PR details
   - Patch summary (files changed)
   - Answer options with descriptions
   - Back button to dashboard

4.4 Keyboard Shortcuts
   Efficient keyboard-driven UX:
   - 1-9: Quick answer selection
   - Enter: Expand/collapse
   - d: Drill-down
   - s: Skip current
   - S: Skip all (with confirm)
   - z: Snooze (1d/7d/30d)
   - t: Insert template
   - b: Bulk apply safe changes
   - a: Apply approved changes
   - p/r: Pause/resume
   - q: Quit

4.5 Snooze Feature
   Defer items without skipping permanently:
   - 1 day / 7 days / 30 days
   - Stored in snoozed.json
   - Checked during discovery

4.6 Response Templates
   Pre-configured responses:
   - stale-issue.md
   - duplicate.md
   - needs-info.md
   - wontfix.md
   - thank-you.md

4.7 Bulk Apply
   Apply all low-risk changes that pass gates:
   - Scan plans for risk_level=low
   - Verify tests passed
   - Verify no unanswered questions
   - Confirm before applying

4.8 Patch Summary View
   Show what changed before approving:
   - Branch name
   - Commit count
   - Files changed with diff stats
   - Test status (PASS/FAIL)
   - gh_actions pending

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Dashboard shows all pending questions
- Drill-down provides full context
- Keyboard shortcuts work reliably
- Snooze persists across sessions
- Templates load and apply
- Bulk apply works for safe changes

Estimated Effort
----------------
~600-800 lines of Bash","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:36.82324859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:36.82324859-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bxii","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.686745523-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vcr9","title":"Implement gh_actions execution from review plan","description":"Task: Implement gh_actions Execution

Purpose
-------
Execute GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels) from the gh_actions
array in review-plan.json during the Apply phase.

gh_actions Schema
-----------------
{
  "gh_actions": [
    {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "body": "Fixed in..."},
    {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "reason": "completed"},
    {"op": "label", "target": "issue#42", "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]},
    {"op": "comment", "target": "pr#15", "body": "Thank you..."}
  ]
}

Supported Operations
--------------------

1. comment
   Add comment to issue or PR
   gh issue comment N --body "..."
   gh pr comment N --body "..."

2. close
   Close issue or PR
   gh issue close N --reason "..."
   gh pr close N --comment "..."

3. label
   Add labels to issue
   gh issue edit N --add-label "label1,label2"

4. (merge - NOT SUPPORTED per policy)

Implementation
--------------

execute_gh_actions()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  local actions
  actions=$(jq -c .gh_actions[] "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  
  while IFS= read -r action; do
    local op target
    op=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .op)
    target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
    
    case "$op" in
      comment) execute_comment "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      close)   execute_close "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      label)   execute_label "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      *)
        log_warn "Unknown gh_action op: $op"
        ;;
    esac
  done <<< "$actions"

execute_comment()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target body type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  body=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .body)
  
  # Parse target: "issue#42" or "pr#15"
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Commenting on $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_close()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target reason type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  reason=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".reason // \"completed\"")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Closing $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --reason "$reason"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --comment "Closing: $reason"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_label()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target labels_json type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  labels_json=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".labels | join(\",\")")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Adding labels to $target: $labels_json"
  
  gh issue edit "$number" -R "$repo_id" --add-label "$labels_json"
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

Action Recording
----------------
Track what was executed for audit:

record_action_executed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/gh_actions.log"
  local ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
  
  echo "${ts}|${repo_id}|${action}" >> "$log_file"

Error Handling
--------------
- Log errors but continue with other actions
- Record failed actions in state
- Retry logic for transient failures
- Skip already-executed actions on resume

Testing
-------
- Mock gh commands for unit tests
- Verify comment body escaping
- Verify close reasons work
- Verify labels applied
- Test error handling

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Comments posted correctly
- [ ] Issues/PRs closed with reason
- [ ] Labels applied
- [ ] Actions logged for audit
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent on retry","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:09.158053493-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented gh_actions execution with 9 functions: execute_gh_actions, execute_gh_action_{comment,close,label}, gh_action_already_executed, record_gh_action_log, parse_gh_action_target, canonicalize_gh_action, get_gh_actions_log_file. Features idempotence, audit logging, policy enforcement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vcr9","depends_on_id":"bd-ldyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.518493177-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vp2","title":"E2E: ru doctor workflow (diagnostics, exit codes)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:10:46.752673805-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T21:16:59.31292603-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T21:16:59.31292603-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive E2E test suite for ru doctor workflow with 14 test cases covering: basic functionality, git/gh/config/repos/projects checks, exit codes (0/3), output format, and status indicators"}
-{"id":"bd-xcj6","title":"Implement JSON output mode for review command","description":"# Task: Implement JSON Output Mode

## Purpose
Add --json flag to `ru review` for machine-readable output, following the ru convention of structured stdout and human-readable stderr.

## Background: ru Output Convention
From AGENTS.md:
- stderr: All human-readable output (progress, errors, summary, help)
- stdout: Only structured output (JSON in --json mode, paths otherwise)

## JSON Output Schema

### Discovery Output (--dry-run --json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "discovery",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "summary": {
    "repos_scanned": 50,
    "items_found": 23,
    "by_priority": {"critical": 2, "high": 5, "normal": 12, "low": 4},
    "by_type": {"issues": 18, "prs": 5}
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "repo": "owner/repo",
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Bug in auth",
      "priority": "high",
      "score": 85,
      "labels": ["bug", "security"],
      "created_at": "2024-12-15T...",
      "updated_at": "2025-01-01T..."
    }
  ]
}
```

### Session Status Output (--json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "plan",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "sessions": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "state": "generating",
      "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
      "items_processing": 3
    },
    "owner/repo2": {
      "state": "waiting",
      "wait_reason": "ask_user_question",
      "question_id": "q123"
    }
  },

    {
      "id": "q123",

      "prompt": "Should I refactor?",

    }
  ],

    "sessions_pending": 1,
    "questions_pending": 1,
    "questions_answered": 3
  }
}
```

### Completion Output (--json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "plan",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "complete",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "summary": {
    "repos_reviewed": 4,
    "items_processed": 12,
    "items_fixed": 8,
    "items_skipped": 3,
    "items_needs_info": 1,
    "commits_created": 15,
    "questions_asked": 5,
    "duration_seconds": 847
  },

      "status": "complete",
      "plan_file": "/path/to/.ru/review-plan.json",
      "commits": 4,
      "items_processed": 3
    }
  }
}
```

### Apply Output (--apply --json)
```json
{
  "command": "review",
  "mode": "apply",
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "status": "complete",
  "applied": {
    "owner/repo1": {
      "pushed": true,
      "commits": 4,
      "gh_actions_executed": [
        {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "success": true},
        {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "success": true}
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Implementation

### JSON Mode Flag
```bash
# In parse_review_args()
--json) REVIEW_JSON_OUTPUT=true ;;
```

### Output Router
```bash
review_output() {
    local type="$1"
    local data="$2"
    
    if [[ "$REVIEW_JSON_OUTPUT" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "$data"  # stdout for JSON
    else
        echo "$data" >&2  # stderr for human
    fi
}
```

### JSON Builders
```bash
build_discovery_json() { ... }
build_status_json() { ... }
build_completion_json() { ... }
build_apply_json() { ... }
```

## Integration Points
- Discovery phase outputs items JSON
- Session monitor outputs status JSON on refresh
- Completion outputs summary JSON
- Apply phase outputs actions JSON

## Testing
- Verify JSON is valid (jq parses it)
- Verify stderr has human output in --json mode
- Verify stdout is empty without --json
- Verify all schema fields present

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --json flag accepted
- [ ] Discovery JSON output correct
- [ ] Status JSON output correct
- [ ] Completion JSON output correct
- [ ] Apply JSON output correct
- [ ] Human output goes to stderr in JSON mode

+{"id":"bd-xcj6","title":"Implement JSON output mode for review command","description":"# Task: Implement JSON Output Mode

## Purpose
Add --json flag to `ru review` for machine-readable output, following the ru convention of structured stdout and human-readable stderr.

## Background: ru Output Convention
From AGENTS.md:
- stderr: All human-readable output (progress, errors, summary, help)
- stdout: Only structured output (JSON in --json mode, paths otherwise)

 {"id":"bd-xdrt","title":"Phase 3: ntm Integration (Robot Mode, Activity Detection)","description":"Phase 3: ntm Integration

Overview
--------
Integrate with ntm (Named Tmux Manager) robot mode for advanced orchestration
capabilities: activity detection, health monitoring, and question routing.

Why ntm?
--------
ntm provides capabilities beyond basic tmux:
- Velocity-based activity detection (chars/sec)
- Health monitoring with error pattern detection
- Robot mode JSON API for programmatic control
- Workflow pipelines for complex multi-step tasks
- Session management and routing

Components
----------

3.1 ntm Driver Implementation
   Implement the unified driver interface using ntm robot mode API:
   - ntm --robot-spawn: Create sessions
   - ntm --robot-status: Query session state
   - ntm --robot-health: Monitor session health
   - ntm --robot-route: Get best pane for new work
   - ntm --robot-send: Deliver messages to sessions

3.2 Activity State Detection
   ntm detects Claude state via output velocity:
   - GENERATING: velocity > 10 chars/sec (active output)
   - WAITING: velocity < 1 chars/sec + prompt pattern
   - THINKING: low velocity, no prompt
   - STALLED: velocity == 0 for > 30 seconds
   - ERROR: error patterns detected

3.3 Wait Reason Extraction
   When WAITING, determine WHY:
   - ask_user_question: AskUserQuestion tool detected
   - agent_question_text: Question in plain text
   - external_prompt: Git/SSH/auth prompt
   - unknown: At prompt, reason unclear

3.4 Health Monitoring
   Monitor session health continuously:
   - Detect rate limits (429, quota exceeded)
   - Detect crashes (panic, SIGSEGV)
   - Detect stalls (no output for extended period)
   - Trigger recovery actions

3.5 Workflow Pipeline
   Define github-review.yaml workflow:
   - verify_prerequisites: Check gh auth
   - understand_codebase: Create/update digest
   - review_issues_prs: Main review work
   - finalize_artifacts: Validate plan created

3.6 Rate-Limit Governor
   Adaptive concurrency based on real limits:
   - Query GitHub API for remaining quota
   - Detect model rate limits from 429s
   - Adjust parallelism dynamically
   - Circuit breaker for cascading failures

Exit Criteria
-------------
- ntm driver passes all interface tests
- Activity detection works reliably
- Wait reasons extracted correctly
- Health monitoring catches common issues
- Rate-limit governor adjusts concurrency

Estimated Effort
----------------
~400 lines Bash + ~300 lines in ntm (Go)
